rule: Raw API key access — use a config module to centralize secret management

translations.technical: Raw API key access — use a config module to centralize secret management
translations.plain_language_single: An API key in {label} is accessed directly from code rather than from a secure configuration. This increases the risk of accidental exposure.
translations.plain_language_multi: API keys are accessed directly from code in {N} places rather than from secure configuration.

education.why_it_matters: API keys accessed directly in code (rather than through secure configuration) are easier to accidentally expose in error logs, version control, or shared environments.
education.what_could_happen: During debugging or code sharing, the key is visible in plain text and can be copied and misused.
education.what_to_do: Ask your developer to use environment variable configuration for all API keys.

business_impact: Keys in code are easier to leak through logs, shares, or screenshots.
consequence: Leaked keys can be abused until they are rotated.

affected_nodes.detail: Raw API key access — use a config module to centralize secret management
affected_nodes.plain_language: An API key in generateChatCompletion is accessed directly from code rather than from a secure configuration. This increases the risk of accidental exposure.

remediation: Move API keys to a config module or environment abstraction; avoid process.env in provider call sites.
